name: Colima (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Colima centred.
history: Colima has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms feature the indigenous symbol that gives Colima its name — interpreted as the arm of a warrior or a depiction of the Volcan de Colima — placed within a quartered field that recalls the Pre-Columbian, Spanish colonial, independent and revolutionary periods of the state's history.
